title: Warhammer Quest - Let’s Play - Part 9
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Nh7jDVmtg0I

Daniel (@dungeondive) brings the Dragon's Eye quest to its dramatic conclusion. The fire chasm is packed with enemies—four skeletons, two orc swordsmen, three orc archers, and a scaled minotaur standing guard by the dragon statue. Both warriors fail their fear checks against the skeletons, taking a -1 to hit penalty, but the Ensorcelled Blade compensates perfectly.

What follows is arguably the best turn in the entire campaign. Harlan goes berserk and chains four consecutive deathblows—skeleton, skeleton, orc, and a final skeleton—clearing half the room in a single activation. Saul follows with the Talon of Death and Amulet of Purity, wiping out the remaining infantry. Six enemies fall in one round. Hope surges. Then the minotaur strikes Saul for seven wounds, killing the witch hunter outright. Harlan stands alone against the beast and three archers. He goes berserk again, lands both attacks on the minotaur, and drops it. But the orc archers roll hot—both hit, dealing enough combined damage to kill the barbarian. Harlan falls within arm's reach of the dragon statue, the crystal gem still clutched in his hand. Daniel gives him one final narrative roll—a six to hurl the gem into the dragon's eye with his dying breath. He rolls. It's not a six. The gem tumbles into the chasm, the curse endures, and the quest ends in glorious failure.

----

What's the closest you've come to completing a quest in Warhammer Quest only to have it snatched away at the very last moment?
